records.chapter: Reward of ten virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from Sa’dan b. Muslim and his name is Abdulrahman b. Muslim from al-Fadhl b. Yasar from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “There are ten such virtues that one who meets God possessing them will go to Paradise. (They are) bearing witness that there is no God except Allah and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, having faith in what Muhammad brought from Allah, establishing prayers, payment of Zakat, fasting in the month of Ramadan, Hajj of the House of Allah, faith and love of the friends of Allah, condemning the enemies of God and abstinence from all intoxicants.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ سَعْدَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ وَ اسْمُهُ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ یَسَارٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: عَشْرُ خِصَالٍ مَنْ لَقِیَ اللَّهَ بِهِنَّ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ شَهَادَهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَ الْإِقْرَارُ بِمَا جَاءَ بِهِ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ وَ إِقَامُ الصَّلَاهِ وَ إِیتَاءُ الزَّکَاهِ وَ صَوْمُ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ وَ حِجُّ الْبَیْتِ وَ الْوَلَایَهُ لِأَوْلِیَاءِ اللَّهِ وَ الْبَرَاءَهُ مِنْ أَعْدَاءِ اللَّهِ وَ اجْتِنَابُ کُلِّ مُسْکِرٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from Sa’dan b. Muslim and his name is Abdulrahman b. Muslim from al-Fadhl b. Yasar from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “There are ten such virtues that one who meets God possessing them will go to Paradise. (They are) bearing witness that there is no God except Allah and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, having faith in what Muhammad brought from Allah, establishing prayers, payment of Zakat, fasting in the month of Ramadan, Hajj of the House of Allah, faith and love of the friends of Allah, condemning the enemies of God and abstinence from all intoxicants.”

records.chapter: Reward of acknowledging the Lordship of Allah, Prophethood of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.), Imamate of Ali (a.s.) and fulfilling religious obligations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated from Mufaddal bin Umar that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “The Almighty Allah has guaranteed a thing for a believer.” Mufaddal asked, “O master! What is that?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “One who has belief in the Lordship of Allah, prophethood of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.), imamate of Ali (a.s.),  fulfills  the  obligatory  deeds  Allah  would  keep  him near Himself forever and He won’t remain hidden from that person.” Mufaddal said, “By God, this grace is not like that of  humans.”  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “You  can  earn many rewards even if you perform a few good deeds.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْأَسَدِیُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُوسَی بْنُ عِمْرَانَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی ضَمِنَ لِلْمُؤْمِنِ ضَمَاناً قَالَ قُلْتُ وَ مَا هُوَ قَالَ ضَمِنَ لَهُ إِنْ هُوَ أَقَرَّ لَهُ بِالرُّبُوبِیَّهِ وَ لِمُحَمَّدٍ ص بِالنُّبُوَّهِ وَ لِعَلِیٍّ ع بِالْإِمَامَهِ وَ أَدَّی مَا افْتَرَضَ اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ أَنْ یُسْکِنَهُ فِی جِوَارِهِ وَ لَمْ یَحْتَجِبْ عَنْهُ قَالَ قُلْتُ فَهَذِهِ وَ اللَّهِ الْکَرَامَهُ الَّتِی لَا یُشْبِهُهَا کَرَامَهٌ وَ هِیَ کَرَامَهُ الْآدَمِیِّینَ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع اعْمَلُوا قَلِیلًا تَنَعَّمُوا کَثِیراً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It is narrated from Mufaddal bin Umar that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The Almighty Allah has guaranteed a thing for a believer.” Mufaddal asked, “O master! What is that?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “One who has belief in the Lordship of Allah, prophethood of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.), imamate of Ali (a.s.),  fulfills  the  obligatory  deeds  Allah  would  keep  him near Himself forever and He won’t remain hidden from that person.” Mufaddal said, “By God, this grace is not like that of  humans.”  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “You  can  earn many rewards even if you perform a few good deeds.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Bismillaah before entering toilet
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  and  his predecessors that Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said, “Whenever you bare yourself (go to toilet) recite Bismillaah for if you do so Satan will keep his eyes closed until you have covered yourself.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع إِذَا تَکَشَّفَ أَحَدُکُمْ لِبَوْلٍ أَوْ غَیْرِ ذَلِکَ فَلْیَقُلْ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ الشَّیْطَانَ یَغُضُّ بَصَرَهُ حَتَّی یَفْرُغَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  and  his predecessors that Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Whenever you bare yourself (go to toilet) recite Bismillaah for if you do so Satan will keep his eyes closed until you have covered yourself.”

records.chapter: Reward of uttering a Name of Allah while performing ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Ja’far b. Muhammad b. Masroor who said it was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Muhammad b. ’Amir from his uncle Abdillah b. ’Amir from Muhammad b. Ismail from Ali b. al-Hakam from Dawud al-’Ijli from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who utters a name of Allah while performing ablution his whole body will become pure. All his sins between two ablutions will be wiped off. One who does not utter a name of Allah while performing ablution only the parts of body concerned with ablution will become pure and not his whole body.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَسْرُورٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ عَنْ عَمِّهِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ دَاوُدَ الْعِجْلِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ فَذَکَرَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ طَهُرَ جَمِیعُ جَسَدِهِ وَ کَانَ الْوُضُوءُ إِلَی الْوُضُوءِ کَفَّارَهً لِمَا بَیْنَهُمَا مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ وَ مَنْ لَمْ یُسَمِّ لَمْ یَطْهُرْ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ إِلَّا مَا أَصَابَهُ الْمَاءُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Ja’far b. Muhammad b. Masroor who said it was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Muhammad b. ’Amir from his uncle Abdillah b. ’Amir from Muhammad b. Ismail from Ali b. al-Hakam from Dawud al-’Ijli from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who utters a name of Allah while performing ablution his whole body will become pure. All his sins between two ablutions will be wiped off. One who does not utter a name of Allah while performing ablution only the parts of body concerned with ablution will become pure and not his whole body.”

records.chapter: Reward of uttering a Name of Allah while performing ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muawiyah b. Hakeem from Abdillah b. al-Mughira from Abdillah b. Muskan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A person who utters the name of Allah while performing ablution is like one who has performed a Ghusl.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ حُکَیْمٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغِیرَهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْکَانَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ ذَکَرَ اللَّهَ عَلَی وُضُوئِهِ فَکَأَنَّمَا اغْتَسَلَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muawiyah b. Hakeem from Abdillah b. al-Mughira from Abdillah b. Muskan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A person who utters the name of Allah while performing ablution is like one who has performed a Ghusl.”

records.chapter: Reward of performing ablution like Amirul Momineen (a.s.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText:
1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ali b. Ahmad al-Wasiti from his uncle Abdulrahman b. Katheer al-Hashimi, the slave of Muhammad b. Ali, from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: "Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) was sitting one day with Ibn al-Hanafiyyah, and he said, "O Muhammad, bring me a vessel with water in it, so I can perform ablution for prayer." Muhammad brought the water, and he (Amir al-Mu'minin) washed his right hand with his left hand, then he said, "In the name of Allah, praise be to Allah who has made water pure and not impure."
He then performed istinja (cleaning the private parts) and said, "O Allah, protect my private parts, forgive me, cover my shame (nakedness), and prohibit the Hellfire from me." Afterward, he rinsed his mouth and said, "O Allah, grant me the ability to argue my case on the day I meet You and let my tongue speak with Your remembrance."
He then sniffed water into his nose and said, "O Allah, do not deprive me of the scent of Paradise and make me among those who enjoy its fragrance, its scent, its aroma, and its perfume."
Next, he washed his face and said, "O Allah, whiten my face on the day when faces will turn black, and do not make my face black on the day when faces will turn white."
He then washed his right hand and said, "O Allah, grant me my book (of deeds) in my right hand and grant me eternity in the Gardens on my left, and make my reckoning an easy one."
Afterward, he washed his left hand and said, "O Allah, do not give my book in my left hand, and do not make it tied to my neck, and I seek refuge in You from the severing of the Fire."
He then wiped his head and said, "O Allah, shower me with Your mercy, blessings, and forgiveness."
Then, he wiped his feet and said, "O Allah, make me steadfast on the Straight Path on the day when feet will slip, and let my effort please You, O Most Merciful of those who are merciful."
Then he raised his head and looked at Muhammad and said, "O Muhammad, whoever performs ablution like my ablution and says like my saying, Allah, the Mighty and Sublime, creates an angel from every drop, who sanctifies it, praises it, and magnifies it. Allah, the Exalted, records a reward for that until the Day of Resurrection."
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ الْوَاسِطِیِّ عَنْ عَمِّهِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ کَثِیرٍ الْهَاشِمِیِّ مَوْلَی مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: بَیْنَا أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع ذَاتَ یَوْمٍ جَالِساً مَعَ ابْنِ الْحَنَفِیَّهِ إِذْ قَالَ یَا مُحَمَّدُ ائْتِنِی بِإِنَاءٍ فِیهِ مَاءٌ أَتَوَضَّأْ لِلصَّلَاهِ فَأَتَاهُ مُحَمَّدٌ بِالْمَاءِ فَأَکْفَأَ بِیَدِهِ الْیُمْنَی عَلَی یَدِهِ الْیُسْرَی ثُمَّ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِی جَعَلَ الْمَاءَ طَهُوراً وَ لَمْ یَجْعَلْهُ نَجِساً قَالَ ثُمَّ اسْتَنْجَی فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ حَصِّنْ فَرْجِی وَ أَعِفَّهُ وَ اسْتُرْ عَوْرَتِی وَ حَرِّمْنِی عَلَی النَّارِ ثُمَّ تَمَضْمَضَ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ لَقِّنِّی حُجَّتِی یَوْمَ أَلْقَاکَ وَ أَطْلِقْ لِسَانِی بِذِکْرِکَ ثُمَّ اسْتَنْشَقَ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ لَا تُحَرِّمْ عَلَیَّ رِیحَ الْجَنَّهِ وَ اجْعَلْنِی مِمَّنْ یَشَمُّ رِیحَهَا وَ رَوْحَهَا وَ رَیْحَانَهَا وَ طِیبَهَا قَالَ ثُمَّ غَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ بَیِّضْ وَجْهِی یَوْمَ تَسْوَدُّ فِیهِ الْوُجُوهُ وَ لَا تُسَوِّدْ وَجْهِی یَوْمَ تَبْیَضُّ فِیهِ الْوُجُوهُ ثُمَّ غَسَلَ یَدَهُ الْیُمْنَی فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ أَعْطِنِی کِتَابِی بِیَمِینِی وَ الْخُلْدَ فِی الْجِنَانِ بِیَسَارِی وَ حَاسِبْنِی حِساباً یَسِیراً ثُمَّ غَسَلَ یَدَهُ الْیُسْرَی فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ لَا تُعْطِنِی کِتَابِی بِشِمَالِی وَ لَا تَجْعَلْهَا مَغْلُولَهً إِلَی عُنُقِی وَ أَعُوذُ بِکَ مِنْ مُقَطَّعَاتِ النِّیرَانِ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ رَأْسَهُ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ غَشِّنِی بِرَحْمَتِکَ وَ بَرَکَاتِکَ وَ عَفْوِکَ قَالَ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ رِجْلَیْهِ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ ثَبِّتْنِی عَلَی الصِّرَاطِ یَوْمَ تَزِلُّ فِیهِ الْأَقْدَامُ وَ اجْعَلْ سَعْیِی فِیمَا یُرْضِیکَ عَنِّی یَا أَرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِینَ ثُمَّ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ فَنَظَرَ إِلَی مُحَمَّدٍ فَقَالَ یَا مُحَمَّدُ مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ مِثْلَ وُضُوئِی وَ قَالَ مِثْلَ قَوْلِی خَلَقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ کُلِّ قَطْرَهٍ مَلَکاً یُقَدِّسُهُ وَ یُسَبِّحُهُ وَ یُکَبِّرُهُ وَ یَکْتُبُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَی لَهُ ثَوَابَ ذَلِکَ إِلَی یَوْمِ الْقِیَامَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ali b. Ahmad al-Wasiti from his uncle Abdulrahman b. Katheer al-Hashimi, the slave of Muhammad b. Ali, from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) was sitting one day with Ibn al-Hanafiyyah, and he said, "O Muhammad, bring me a vessel with water in it, so I can perform ablution for prayer." Muhammad brought the water, and he (Amir al-Mu'minin) washed his right hand with his left hand, then he said, "In the name of Allah, praise be to Allah who has made water pure and not impure."
He then performed istinja (cleaning the private parts) and said, "O Allah, protect my private parts, forgive me, cover my shame (nakedness), and prohibit the Hellfire from me." Afterward, he rinsed his mouth and said, "O Allah, grant me the ability to argue my case on the day I meet You and let my tongue speak with Your remembrance."
He then sniffed water into his nose and said, "O Allah, do not deprive me of the scent of Paradise and make me among those who enjoy its fragrance, its scent, its aroma, and its perfume."
Next, he washed his face and said, "O Allah, whiten my face on the day when faces will turn black, and do not make my face black on the day when faces will turn white."
He then washed his right hand and said, "O Allah, grant me my book (of deeds) in my right hand and grant me eternity in the Gardens on my left, and make my reckoning an easy one."
Afterward, he washed his left hand and said, "O Allah, do not give my book in my left hand, and do not make it tied to my neck, and I seek refuge in You from the severing of the Fire."
He then wiped his head and said, "O Allah, shower me with Your mercy, blessings, and forgiveness."
Then, he wiped his feet and said, "O Allah, make me steadfast on the Straight Path on the day when feet will slip, and let my effort please You, O Most Merciful of those who are merciful."
Then he raised his head and looked at Muhammad and said, "O Muhammad, whoever performs ablution like my ablution and says like my saying, Allah, the Mighty and Sublime, creates an angel from every drop, who sanctifies it, praises it, and magnifies it. Allah, the Exalted, records a reward for that until the Day of Resurrection."

records.chapter: Reward when the parts of body concerned with ablution are wiped and when they are not
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Ibrahim b. Muhammad al-Thaqafi from Ali b. Ya’la from Ibrahim b. Muhammad b. Hamran from his father from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A person who wipes the parts of body after ablution will get a single reward and the person who does not wipe will get thirty rewards.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ سَلَمَهَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الثَّقَفِیِّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ یَعْلَی عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حُمْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ وَ تَمَنْدَلَ کُتِبَتْ لَهُ حَسَنَهٌ وَ مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ وَ لَمْ یَتَمَنْدَلْ حَتَّی تَجِفَّ وَضُوؤُهُ کُتِبَتْ لَهُ ثَلَاثُونَ حَسَنَهً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Ibrahim b. Muhammad al-Thaqafi from Ali b. Ya’la from Ibrahim b. Muhammad b. Hamran from his father from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A person who wipes the parts of body after ablution will get a single reward and the person who does not wipe will get thirty rewards.”

records.chapter: Reward of ablution of Maghrib and Morning Prayers
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) says, “The ablution of Maghrib compensates for the sins of a person committed during that day  excluding  the  greater  sins.  The  ablution  of  Morning Prayer  compensates  for  the  sins  of  a  person  committed during the previous night excluding the greater sins.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَمْرَهَ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ جَرَّاحٍ الْحَذَّاءِ عَنْ سَمَاعَهَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ مُوسَی ع مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ لِلْمَغْرِبِ کَانَ وُضُوؤُهُ ذَلِکَ کَفَّارَهً لِمَا مَضَی مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ فِی نَهَارِهِ مَا خَلَا الْکَبَائِرَ وَ مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ لِصَلَاهِ الصُّبْحِ کَانَ وُضُوؤُهُ ذَلِکَ کَفَّارَهً لِمَا مَضَی مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ فِی لَیْلَتِهِ مَا خَلَا الْکَبَائِرَ.

records.chapter: Reward of keeping eyes open during ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ridha  (a.s.)  says,  “Keep  your  eyes  open  while performing  ablution.  Perhaps  you  will  save  yourself  from seeing the fire of Hell by doing so.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ أَبِی حَمَّادٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ غَزْوَانَ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنِ ابْنِ جَرِیحٍ عَنْ عَطَاءٍ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص افْتَحُوا عُیُونَکُمْ عِنْدَ الْوُضُوءِ لَعَلَّهَا لَا تَرَی نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ.

records.chapter: Reward of repeating ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh from Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad b. al-Saqr from Abi Qatadah from al-Ridha (a.s.) who said: “Repeating ablution for Isha prayer wipes the sins concerning oaths (yes, by Allah; no, by Allah)”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الصَّقْرِ عَنْ أَبِی قَتَادَهَ عَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ: تَجْدِیدُ الْوُضُوءِ لِصَلَاهِ الْعِشَاءِ یَمْحُو لَا وَ اللَّهِ وَ بَلَی وَ اللَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh from Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad b. al-Saqr from Abi Qatadah from al-Ridha (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Repeating ablution for Isha prayer wipes the sins concerning oaths (yes, by Allah; no, by Allah)”

records.chapter: Reward of repeating ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from his father from Muhammad b. Sinan from al-Muffadhal b. Umar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who repeats ablution apart from prayers, the Almighty Allah forgives all his sin even without repentance.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ جَدَّدَ وُضُوءَهُ لِغَیْرِ صَلَاهٍ جَدَّدَ اللَّهُ تَوْبَتَهُ مِنْ غَیْرِ اسْتِغْفَارٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from his father from Muhammad b. Sinan from al-Muffadhal b. Umar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who repeats ablution apart from prayers, the Almighty Allah forgives all his sin even without repentance.”

records.chapter: Reward of doing Miswak
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Ahmad b. Idris who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad who said it was narrated to me by Ibrahim b. Ishaq from Muhammad b. Isa from ’Ubayd-Allah al-Dahqan from Durust b. Mansur from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “There are twelve virtues of doing Miswak – it is the Sunnah of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.), it cleans the mouth, improves eyesight, Allah becomes pleased, teeth are cleansed, teeth are prevented from decay, jaws become stronger, it increases appetite, it removes phlegm, it improves memory, it doubles the good deeds of a person and angels become happy.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی إِبْرَاهِیمُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ عُبَیْدِ اللَّهِ الدِّهْقَانِ عَنْ دُرُسْتَ بْنِ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: فِی السِّوَاکِ اثْنَتَا عَشْرَهَ خَصْلَهً هُوَ مِنَ السُّنَّهِ وَ مَطْهَرَهٌ لِلْفَمِ وَ مَجْلَاهٌ لِلْبَصَرِ وَ یُرْضِی الرَّحْمَنَ وَ یُبَیِّضُ الْأَسْنَانَ وَ یَذْهَبُ بِالْحَفْرِ وَ یَشُدُّ اللِّثَهَ وَ یُشَهِّی الطَّعَامَ وَ یَذْهَبُ بِالْبَلْغَمِ وَ یَزِیدُ فِی الْحِفْظِ وَ یُضَاعِفُ الْحَسَنَاتِ وَ تَفْرَحُ بِهِ الْمَلَائِکَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Ahmad b. Idris who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad who said it was narrated to me by Ibrahim b. Ishaq from Muhammad b. Isa from ’Ubayd-Allah al-Dahqan from Durust b. Mansur from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “There are twelve virtues of doing Miswak – it is the Sunnah of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.), it cleans the mouth, improves eyesight, Allah becomes pleased, teeth are cleansed, teeth are prevented from decay, jaws become stronger, it increases appetite, it removes phlegm, it improves memory, it doubles the good deeds of a person and angels become happy.”

records.chapter: Reward of doing Miswak
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  that  Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) said, “If people would have known the benefit of doing Miswak they would have done it even on their beds.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ سَعِیدٍ الْمَدَائِنِیِّ عَنْ مُصَدِّقِ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنْ عَمَّارٍ السَّابَاطِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع لَوْ یَعْلَمُ النَّاسُ مَا فِی السِّوَاکِ لَأَبَاتُوهُ مَعَهُمْ فِی اللِّحَافِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  that  Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If people would have known the benefit of doing Miswak they would have done it even on their beds.”

records.chapter: Reward of doing Miswak
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. Abi (rah) from Abdillah b. Ja’far from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from Safwan b. Yahya from Ibrahim b. Abi al-Balad from his father Yahya b. Abi al-Balad from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Doing Miswak removes phlegm and increases intelligence.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ أَبِی الْبِلَادِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ یَحْیَی بْنِ أَبِی الْبِلَادِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: السِّوَاکُ یَذْهَبُ بِالْبَلْغَمِ وَ یَزِیدُ فِی الْعَقْلِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. Abi (rah) from Abdillah b. Ja’far from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from Safwan b. Yahya from Ibrahim b. Abi al-Balad from his father Yahya b. Abi al-Balad from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Doing Miswak removes phlegm and increases intelligence.”

records.chapter: Swallowing ones spit for the respect of mosque
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  that  the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who swallows his spit  out  of  respect  of mosque  Allah  makes  his  mouth  the cause of safety of his body and no harm would be caused to his body.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنِ السِّنْدِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ طَلْحَهَ بْنِ زَیْدٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ رَدَّ رِیقَهُ تَعْظِیماً لِحَقِّ الْمَسْجِدِ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ رِیقَهُ صِحَّهً فِی بَدَنِهِ وَ عُوفِیَ مِنْ بَلْوَی فِی جَسَدِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  that  the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who swallows his spit  out  of  respect  of mosque  Allah  makes  his  mouth  the cause of safety of his body and no harm would be caused to his body.”

records.chapter: Swallowing ones spit for the respect of mosque
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. Abi (rah) from Abdillah b. Ja’far from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Ahmad from his father [from] Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who does not spit in mosque and swallows it, would soon be cured of the disease from which he is suffering.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ [عَنْ] عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ تَنَخَّعَ فِی مَسْجِدٍ ثُمَّ رَدَّهَا فِی جَوْفِهِ لَمْ تَمُرَّ بِدَاءٍ إِلَّا أَبْرَأَتْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. Abi (rah) from Abdillah b. Ja’far from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Ahmad from his father [from] Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who does not spit in mosque and swallows it, would soon be cured of the disease from which he is suffering.”

records.chapter: Reward of performing ablution before going to bed
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Sindi b. Rab’a from Muhammad b. Kurdoos from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who goes to bed after performing ablution, his bed is like the place of worship in a mosque the whole night.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ السِّنْدِیِّ بْنِ رَبْعَهَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ کُرْدُوسٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ تَطَهَّرَ ثُمَّ آوَی إِلَی فِرَاشِهِ بَاتَ وَ فِرَاشُهُ کَمَسْجِدِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Sindi b. Rab’a from Muhammad b. Kurdoos from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who goes to bed after performing ablution, his bed is like the place of worship in a mosque the whole night.”

records.chapter: Reward of rinsing one’s mouth and nose often
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  says,  “For  one  who  rinses  his mouth and nose with water often this act would be a tool of salvation and he would develop a hatred for Shaitaan.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَیْهِمْ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لِیُبَالِغْ أَحَدُکُمْ فِی الْمَضْمَضَهِ وَ الِاسْتِنْشَاقِ فَإِنَّهُ غُفْرَانٌ لَکُمْ وَ مَنْفَرَهٌ لِلشَّیْطَانِ.

records.chapter: Reward of wrapping a cloth while going to bathroom
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Ali b. Ahmad from his father from his grandfather Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from his father from Muhammad b. Khalid and Muhammad b. Sinan from al-Muffadhal b. Umar from al-Sadiq (a.s.) who said: “The Almighty Allah will cover with His Own cover one who goes to bathroom wrapping a cloth around himself.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنِ الصَّادِقِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ دَخَلَ الْحَمَّامَ بِمِئْزَرٍ سَتَرَهُ اللَّهُ بِسِتْرِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Ali b. Ahmad from his father from his grandfather Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from his father from Muhammad b. Khalid and Muhammad b. Sinan from al-Muffadhal b. Umar from al-Sadiq (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The Almighty Allah will cover with His Own cover one who goes to bathroom wrapping a cloth around himself.”

records.chapter: Reward of avoiding the sight of private parts of a believer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Khalid from Muhammad b. Ali al-Ansari from Abdillah b. Muhammad from Abdillah b. Sinan from al-Sadiq (a.s.) who said: “The Almighty Allah will save him from the boiling water of Hell one who goes to the bathroom and avoids seeing private parts of a brother in faith.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْأَنْصَارِیِّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الصَّادِقِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ دَخَلَ الْحَمَّامَ فَغَضَّ طَرْفَهُ عَنِ النَّظَرِ إِلَی عَوْرَهِ أَخِیهِ آمَنَهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْحَمِیمِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Khalid from Muhammad b. Ali al-Ansari from Abdillah b. Muhammad from Abdillah b. Sinan from al-Sadiq (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The Almighty Allah will save him from the boiling water of Hell one who goes to the bathroom and avoids seeing private parts of a brother in faith.”

records.chapter: Reward of washing one’s head with Marshmallow (Khatmi)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from his father from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. Umar from Muhammad b. Sinan from Abi Sa’eed al-Qammat from Umar b. Yazid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Washing head with marsh- mallow (Khatmi) ends headache, diseases and poverty and protects head from dryness.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی سَعِیدٍ الْقَمَّاطِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: غَسْلُ الرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِیِّ أَمَانٌ مِنَ الصُّدَاعِ وَ بَرَاءَهٌ مِنَ الْفَقْرِ وَ طَهُورٌ لِلرَّأْسِ مِنَ الْحَزَازِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from his father from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. Umar from Muhammad b. Sinan from Abi Sa’eed al-Qammat from Umar b. Yazid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Washing head with marsh- mallow (Khatmi) ends headache, diseases and poverty and protects head from dryness.”

records.chapter: Reward of washing one’s head with Marshmallow (Khatmi)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Isa from Abi Ayoob’ al-Madaini from Ibn Abi ’Umayra from Sufyan b. al-Shamt [al-Samt] from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “It is a protection.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ أَبِی أَیُّوبَ الْمَدَائِنِیِّ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ سُفْیَانَ بْنِ الشمط [السِّمْطِ] عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: غَسْلُ الرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِیِّ یَنْفِی الْفَقْرَ وَ یَزِیدُ فِی الرِّزْقِ وَ قَالَ هُوَ نشر [نُشْرَهٌ].
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Isa from Abi Ayoob’ al-Madaini from Ibn Abi ’Umayra from Sufyan b. al-Shamt [al-Samt] from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “It is a protection.”

records.chapter: Reward of washing one’s head with Marshmallow (Khatmi)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3.  Imam  Musa  Kadhim  (a.s.)  says,  “Washing  head  with Khatmi is the best way to increase one’s earnings.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ یُونُسَ بُزُرْجَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ع یَقُولُ غَسْلُ الرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِیِّ یَجْلِبُ الرِّزْقَ جَلْباً.

records.chapter: Reward of washing head with Jujube (Beri) leaves
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) used to wash his head with Jujube leaves and say, “Wash your head with jujube leaves for all the angels and prophets  consider  this  holy.  Allah  will  keep  away  evil thoughts  of  Shaitaan  for  seventy  days  from  the  one  who washes  his  head  with  jujube  leaves.  That  person  won’t commit sins and one who does not commit sins will enter Paradise.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ زَیْدٍ الْبُرْسِیِّ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ کَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص یَغْسِلُ رَأْسَهُ بِالسِّدْرِ وَ یَقُولُ اغْسِلُوا رُءُوسَکُمْ بِوَرَقِ السِّدْرِ فَإِنَّهُ قَدَّسَهُ کُلُّ مَلَکٍ مُقَرَّبٍ وَ کُلُّ نَبِیٍّ مُرْسَلٍ وَ مَنْ غَسَلَ رَأْسَهُ بِوَرَقِ السِّدْرِ صَرَفَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ وَسْوَسَهَ الشَّیْطَانِ سَبْعِینَ یَوْماً وَ مَنْ صَرَفَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ وَسْوَسَهَ الشَّیْطَانِ سَبْعِینَ یَوْماً لَمْ یَعْصِ وَ مَنْ لَمْ یَعْصِ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) used to wash his head with Jujube leaves and say,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Wash your head with jujube leaves for all the angels and prophets  consider  this  holy.  Allah  will  keep  away  evil thoughts  of  Shaitaan  for  seventy  days  from  the  one  who washes  his  head  with  jujube  leaves.  That  person  won’t commit sins and one who does not commit sins will enter Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of washing head with Jujube (Beri) leaves
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  It  is  narrated  from  Isa  bin  Abdullah  Alawi  who  has narrated from his father and he in turn from his father that when  the  Messenger  of  Allah  (s.a.w.s.)  used  to  be  sad, Jibraeel (a.s.) used to come to him and say, “Do wash your head with jujube leaves.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنْ عِیسَی بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْعَلَوِیِّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ص اغْتَمَّ فَأَمَرَهُ جَبْرَئِیلُ ع أَنْ یَغْسِلَ رَأْسَهُ بِالسِّدْرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2.  It  is  narrated  from  Isa  bin  Abdullah  Alawi  who  has narrated from his father and he in turn from his father that when  the  Messenger  of  Allah  (s.a.w.s.)  used  to  be  sad, Jibraeel (a.s.) used to come to him and say,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Do wash your head with jujube leaves.”
